feed.title: [Research] A clockless vardiff strands a slowing miner

entry.title: [Research] A clockless vardiff strands a slowing miner
entry.summary:
When considering adjustments to the hashrate in scenarios where it is stranded, a reduction to 99% rather than completely dropping to 0% presents a more realistic and practical approach. This perspective takes into account that if a miner's hashrate drops entirely to zero, adjusting the difficulty downwards would be ineffectual since there would be no recovery of mining activity from that point. On the other hand, reducing the hashrate by 99% still leaves room for operational activity and the potential recovery of the miner.
Further analysis into this scenario involves assessing the response rate of miners whose hashrates have been significantly reduced. The key measure to observe is the speed at which these miners can return to their target share rate under different algorithm configurations. This examination helps in understanding the resilience and adaptability of mining operations under extreme but plausible conditions that may affect mining efficiency and output.
